news.headline: Yard Force Cable Plug Grass Cleaner now available at Costco Japan - helps maintain pristine lawns effortlessly.
news.summary: Yard Force, a well-known brand in outdoor power equipment, has introduced the Yard Force Cable Plug Grass Cleaner at Costco Japan. This new product aims to provide Japanese homeowners with a convenient solution for lawn maintenance. The Grass Cleaner is designed for efficiency and power, promising to revolutionize the way people care for their lawns. It offers exceptional performance to make tasks easier and more effective. The product is now available for purchase at Costco Japan, offering customers a user-friendly tool to keep their lawns pristine.

news.headline: Apollo Silver Corp. reports AGM results: all 4 directors elected, auditors re-appointed, shareholders approve employee Share Option Plan.
news.summary: In Vancouver, British Columbia, on June 28, 2024, Apollo Silver Corp. disclosed that at its Annual General Meeting, shareholders voted in favor of several key proposals. The shareholders, by a majority, elected all four directors up for election. They also approved the re-appointment of Davidson & Company LLP as auditors of the Company. Additionally, shareholders backed the Company’s employee Share Option Plan. Apollo Silver Corp. is listed as (TSX.V:APGO, OTCQB:APGOF, Frankfurt:6ZF0).

news.headline: Highest value of shares traded on Nasdaq closing cross during 2024 Russell US Indexes reconstitution sets new record.
news.summary: At least 15 people were injured in a car crash on the highway yesterday afternoon. The accident involved three vehicles and occurred near Exit 23. Emergency services arrived at the scene within 10 minutes of receiving the call for help. The injured individuals were taken to two nearby hospitals for treatment, where four of them are reported to be in critical condition. Authorities have launched an investigation to determine the cause of the crash. This incident serves as a reminder for drivers to exercise caution on the roads and adhere to traffic regulations to prevent such accidents.

news.headline: Abacus Life closes public offering of 11.5M shares at $8.00/share, raising $92M gross proceeds, oversubscribed.
news.summary: Orlando-based company Abacus Life, Inc. (NASDAQ: ABL) recently completed a successful public offering, exceeding expectations by closing with 11,500,000 shares of common stock. This included an additional 1,500,000 shares purchased by underwriters at the public offering price of $8.00 per share. The total funds raised from the oversubscribed offering amounted to around $92 million before deducting underwriting discounts, commissions, and estimated expenses. Abacus is known for its focus on longevity and actuarial technology as a leading alternative asset manager.

news.headline: Fed's preferred inflation gauge, PCE index, expected to drop to a 38-month low of 2.6%, signaling potential for a market rally.
news.summary: Traders are getting ready for the upcoming release of the Personal Consumption Expenditure (PCE) price index data, which is considered the Federal Reserve’s preferred measure of inflation. Scheduled for Friday, the Bureau of Economic Analysis will also be releasing data on personal income and personal spending simultaneously. Investors are particularly interested in whether the Fed’s inflation measure will decrease as economists are currently predicting. A decline would reinforce confidence in efforts to steer price pressures towards the 2% target and heighten expectations for future interest rate cuts.According to estimates compiled by Econoday, the PCE price index is anticipated to slow down from 2.7% year-on-year in April to 2.6% year-on-year in May. The consensus range falls between 2.5% and 2.7%. On a monthly basis, the PCE price index is expected to rise at a slower pace of 0.1%, compared to the previous month's 0.3% increase. Furthermore, the core PCE price index is projected to ease from 2.8% to 2.6%. For more detailed information, the full story can be found on Benzinga.com.

news.headline: Teamsters at Gardner Cryogenics secure substantial pay raises and end strike after ratifying first contract with 184 skilled workers.
news.summary: Teamsters Local 773 in Bethlehem, Pennsylvania successfully negotiated significant pay raises for 184 skilled workers at Gardner Cryogenics, bringing an end to a two-week strike. The workers have voted overwhelmingly to ratify their first Teamsters contract, marking a major victory for the union. This development comes after the strike that began on June 28, 2024, demonstrating the effectiveness of collective bargaining in securing improved wages and working conditions for employees.
